summary: Russian forces conducted a large-scale missile and drone strike on Kyiv, killing 13 and injuring 86, with infrastructure damage across multiple cities. The EU announced new sanctions targeting Russian military-industrial companies in response to civilian attacks. Fighting continues along the eastern and northern fronts, with Ukrainian forces repelling assaults near Kharkiv and Sumy. (Source: @eRadarrua/99323)
key_points: Russian forces liberated Piskunovka in the DNR according to TASS, amid ongoing offensive operations in eastern Ukraine. (Source: @tass_agency/383392)

A massive overnight strike on Kyiv involved Iskander ballistic missiles, S-400/Zircon air defense missiles, and over 100 jet-powered drones, complicating Ukrainian air defenses. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31313)
The EU proposed expanded sanctions against Russian military-industrial companies following civilian infrastructure attacks in Kyiv and other cities. (Source: @UkraineNow/82335)
Polish official Marcin Bogucki criticized Ukraine's glorification of UPA figures, linking it to historical crimes in Volyn and Eastern Lesser Poland. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31330)
German prosecutors charged Ukrainian national Sergey Kuznetsov for involvement in the 2022 Nord Stream pipeline sabotage. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31323)
Reactive drones were reported flying from Chernihiv and Sumy regions toward Kyiv, indicating continued aerial threats. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68495)
Russian forces shot down 7 Ukrainian UAVs over Leningrad region and 5 near Moscow, while Crimea experienced blackouts due to drone raids. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Ukrainian forces repelled an armored counterattack on the Kupyansk axis, with small assault groups advancing in urban areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces conducted a large-scale missile and drone strike on Kyiv, killing 13 and injuring 86, with infrastructure damage across multiple cities. The EU announced new sanctions targeting Russian military-industrial companies in response to civilian attacks. Fighting continues along the eastern and northern fronts, with Ukrainian forces repelling assaults near Kharkiv and Sumy. (Source: @eRadarrua/99323)

points: Kyiv suffered 13 deaths and 86 injuries following a Russian missile and drone strike targeting energy facilities, logistics centers, and civilian infrastructure. (Source: @eRadarrua/99323)
Zaporozhye's meat plant was struck by two drones, halting production, while a logistics terminal was damaged in Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @eRadarrua/99357)
Dnipro's transport infrastructure was hit, and Borispyil's Porsche dealership was damaged by fire, destroying two vehicles. (Source: @eRadarrua/99357)
Kyiv's BookChef publishing warehouse burned, destroying 800,000 books, with potential delays in order processing. (Source: @UkraineNow/82344)
Ukrainian forces repelled an armored counterattack on the Kupyansk axis, with assault groups advancing in urban areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Russian forces shot down 7 Ukrainian UAVs over Leningrad region and 5 near Moscow, while Crimea experienced blackouts in Feodosia and Armyansk. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Rylsk in Kursk region reported 6 wounded after Ukrainian strikes, including an OMON explosives technician killed while defusing a drone warhead. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Belgorod region saw multiple drone strikes: a man killed in Malakeyevo, injuries in Zenino, Borisovka, Shebekino, and Volokonovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Russia's Ministry of Health plans to expand neonatal screening to include five additional genetic disorders. (Source: @tass_agency/383362)
Fighting continues in Shostka district (Bachevsk), Sumy district (Pisarevka, Marino, Novaya Sech, Khoten), and Krasnopollye forest areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Ukrainske settlement in Vovchansk district was liberated, with ongoing battles near Losevka cemetery and Zemlyanom Yar. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Heavy offensive battles persist in northern Konstantinovka, while fighting continues on Dobropollye's eastern outskirts. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Krasny Liman remains contested, with Russian forces tracking house-by-house advances. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Reactive drones were reported flying from Chernihiv and Sumy regions toward Kyiv, targeting Brovary and Vyshgorod districts. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68495)
Three reactive drones were detected near Slavutych heading toward Kyiv. (Source: @eRadarrua/99315)
Sumy region residents reported prolonged power and water outages since June 30 amid intensified Russian strikes. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
A threat of ballistic missile use from the south was reported, with a drone group operating over Mykolaiv region from the Black Sea. (Source: @kpszsu/67128)
Gurzuf in Crimea saw the start of the tourist season end abruptly amid ongoing Russian occupation. (Source: @eRadarrua/99360)
Over 60 missiles and 100+ jet-powered drones were used in the Kyiv strike, including Iskander ballistic missiles and S-400/Zircon systems. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31313)
Reactive drones were reported from Chernihiv, Sumy, and near Slavutych, targeting Kyiv. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68495)
Russian forces shot down 25+ drones in Rostov region, with additional downings in Leningrad and Moscow areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)

key_developments: Russian forces liberated Piskunovka in the DNR amid ongoing eastern Ukraine offensives, per TASS. (Source: @tass_agency/383392)
Kyiv strike killed 13, injured 86, with damage to energy, logistics, and publishing infrastructure. (Source: @eRadarrua/99323)
EU proposed sanctions targeting Russian military-industrial companies following civilian attacks. (Source: @UkraineNow/82335)
German prosecutors charged Ukrainian national Kuznetsov for 2022 Nord Stream pipeline sabotage. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31323)
Ukrainian forces repelled armored counterattack on Kupyansk axis, advancing in urban areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Reactive drones targeted Kyiv from Chernihiv and Sumy regions, with multiple launches reported. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68495)
Russia expanded neonatal screening to include five new genetic disorders. (Source: @tass_agency/383362)
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces demonstrated enhanced aerial capabilities with jet-powered drones and mixed missile salvos, complicating Ukrainian air defenses. The use of Iskander ballistic missiles and reactive drones suggests continued escalation in targeting critical infrastructure. Political tensions rose as Poland criticized Ukraine's historical narratives, while Germany pursued legal action over Nord Stream sabotage. These developments indicate sustained operational pressure and diplomatic friction. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31313)
sections.regional_response: The EU's proposed sanctions against Russian military-industrial entities reflect a coordinated Western response to civilian infrastructure attacks. Poland's criticism of Ukraine's UPA-related educational materials highlights divergent historical perspectives among allies. Germany's prosecution of a Ukrainian national for Nord Stream sabotage underscores ongoing legal and political ramifications. These reactions suggest alignment on punitive measures but potential friction over historical narratives. (Source: @UkraineNow/82335)
sections.intelligence_notes: Secret 2025 military exercises between China and Russia in Beijing focused on radiation, chemical, and biological defense, indicating strategic coordination. The use of reactive drones from northern regions suggests logistical adaptation by Russian forces. Ukrainian forces' ability to repel armored counterattacks on the Kupyansk axis may signal tactical resilience. Monitoring cross-border drone activity and international legal proceedings remains critical. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31318)
